entries.title: Kalman Filter
entries.content: An algorithm that uses a series of measurements observed over time to produce estimates of unknown variables.
entries.example: Tracking position of a spacecraft from noisy radar readings

entries.title: Karnaugh Map
entries.content: A method of simplifying Boolean algebra expressions visually without algebraic manipulation.
entries.formula: Grid of cells with gray code indexing
entries.example: Simplifying logic gates count in digital design

entries.title: Kendall's Rank Correlation
entries.content: A non-parametric metric measuring the ordinal association between two quantities.
entries.example: Comparing two rank-ordered lists of search results

entries.title: Kepler's Third Law Formula
entries.content: Mathematical relation stating the square of a planet's orbital period is proportional to the cube of its semi-major axis.
entries.example: Calculating the orbit time of Mars around the Sun

entries.title: Kernel of Homomorphism
entries.content: The set of elements in the domain that map to the identity element of the codomain.
entries.example: Finding null space of group map

entries.title: Kernel of Linear Map
entries.content: The set of vectors that map to the zero vector under a linear transformation, also known as the null space.
entries.example: Solving homogeneous system A*x = 0

entries.title: Kite Area
entries.content: The area of a quadrilateral with two pairs of equal adjacent sides, equal to half the product of its diagonals.
entries.example: A kite with diagonals of 6 cm and 10 cm has area 30 cm^2

entries.title: Klein Bottle
entries.content: A non-orientable one-sided surface with no boundary, which can only be fully embedded in four dimensions.
entries.example: A 3D projection of a bottle that loops back into itself

entries.title: Knot Invariant
entries.content: A property of a mathematical knot that remains unchanged under any ambient isotopy (deformation without cutting).
entries.example: Proving two knots are distinct by calculating invariants

entries.title: Knot Theory Definition
entries.content: The study of mathematical closed curves in three dimensions, analyzing properties of knots.
entries.formula: Knot K in R^3 isomorphic to S^1
entries.example: Classifying different types of closed loop twists

entries.title: Kolmogorov-Smirnov Test
entries.content: A non-parametric test comparing a sample distribution with a reference probability distribution.
entries.example: Testing if a sample dataset comes from a normal distribution

entries.title: Kronecker Delta
entries.content: A function of two variables, usually integers, which is 1 if they are equal, and 0 otherwise.
entries.example: Identity matrix entries represented as delta_ij

entries.title: Kronecker Product
entries.content: An operation on two matrices of arbitrary size resulting in a block matrix.
entries.example: Tensor product of vector states in quantum computing

entries.title: Kruskal's Algorithm
entries.content: A minimum spanning tree algorithm that finds an edge of the least possible weight that connects any two trees in a forest.
entries.example: Finding the cheapest fiber-optic network layout connecting cities

entries.title: Kullback-Leibler Divergence
entries.content: A measure of how one probability distribution diverges from a second, expected probability distribution.
entries.example: Measuring information loss in machine learning models

entries.title: Kurtosis Definition
entries.content: A measure of the 'tailedness' of the probability distribution of a real-valued random variable.
entries.example: Determining if a dataset has fat tails compared to normal distribution

entries.title: K-automorphism
entries.content: An automorphism of a field extension that fixes every element of the base field K.
entries.example: Galois group elements of a polynomial splitting field

entries.title: K-theory Algebraic
entries.content: A branch of algebra studying vector bundles and projective modules on rings using K-groups.
entries.example: Calculating topological invariants of ring structures

entries.title: K-theory Topological
entries.content: A tool in algebraic topology using vector bundles to define generalized cohomology theories.
entries.example: Classifying vector bundles over a sphere

entries.title: K-connectivity Graph
entries.content: The minimum number of vertices whose removal disconnects the graph or leaves a single vertex.
entries.example: A cycle graph C_4 has vertex connectivity 2

entries.title: K-nearest Neighbors Math
entries.content: A classification algorithm that classifies a point based on the majority vote of its k closest neighbors.
entries.example: Classifying a new data point based on 5 nearest neighbors

entries.title: Koch Snowflake Area
entries.content: The area of the Koch snowflake fractal, which is 8/5 times the area of the initial triangle.
entries.example: Area of snowflake starting with equilateral triangle of area 10

entries.title: Koch Snowflake Perimeter
entries.content: The perimeter of the Koch snowflake fractal, which is infinite.
entries.example: A shape with finite area but infinite perimeter boundary

entries.title: Konigsberg Bridge Problem
entries.content: A historical problem solved by Euler, leading to the foundation of graph theory.
entries.formula: Degree of vertices must be even for Eulerian path
entries.example: Attempting to cross 7 bridges in a city without repeating

entries.title: K-simplex Topology
entries.content: A generalization of a triangle or tetrahedron to arbitrary dimensions.
entries.formula: S = convex hull of k+1 vertices
entries.example: A 0-simplex is a point, 1-simplex is segment, 2-simplex is triangle

entries.title: Kepler Conjecture
entries.content: A mathematical theorem about sphere packing in three-dimensional Euclidean space.
entries.example: Packing oranges in a grocery display optimally

entries.title: Killing Form Lie Algebra
entries.content: A symmetric bilinear form on a Lie algebra, invariant under automorphisms.
entries.example: Classifying semisimple Lie algebras

entries.title: Kleene Closure
entries.content: The set of all strings of symbols that can be generated by concatenating elements of a set, including empty string.
entries.example: Generating valid strings in a formal language alphabet

entries.title: Klein Four-Group
entries.content: The smallest non-cyclic group, isomorphic to the direct product of two cyclic groups of order 2.
entries.formula: V_4 = {e, a, b, c} with a^2=e, ab=c
entries.example: Symmetries of a non-square rectangle

entries.title: Knot Crossing Number
entries.content: The minimum number of crossings in any projection of a mathematical knot.
entries.example: The unknot has crossing number 0; trefoil has 3

entries.title: Knot Unknot
entries.content: The simplest knot, which is a simple closed loop without any twists or crossings.
entries.example: A circle of rope lying untangled on the floor

entries.title: Knot Prime
entries.content: A knot that cannot be decomposed into the sum of two non-trivial knots.
entries.example: The trefoil knot is a prime knot

entries.title: Knot Trefoil
entries.content: The simplest non-trivial knot, having three crossings.
entries.example: A classic overhand knot with ends spliced together

entries.title: Knot Figure Eight
entries.content: The unique prime knot with crossing number four.
entries.example: The second simplest non-trivial knot

entries.title: Kronecker Symbol
entries.content: A generalization of the Jacobi symbol to all integers.
entries.example: Analyzing quadratic residues in number theory

entries.title: Kruskal-Wallis Test
entries.content: A non-parametric method for testing whether samples originate from the same distribution.
entries.example: Comparing test scores from three different classes

entries.title: Kummer's Theorem
entries.content: Formula for the exponent of a prime dividing a binomial coefficient.
entries.formula: Exponent = carry counts in base p addition
entries.example: Finding powers of 2 in 10C5

entries.title: K-basis
entries.content: A basis of a vector space over the field K.
entries.formula: Basis vectors linear combination coefficients in K
entries.example: Standard R basis for vector coordinates

entries.title: K-dimension
entries.content: The dimension of a vector space over the field K.
entries.example: Dimension of complex numbers as a real vector space is 2

entries.title: Kummer's Congruence
entries.content: A congruence relation between values of the Bernoulli numbers.
entries.example: Number theory congruence relations of bernoulli values
